title: Electrical safety triage notes (visual red flags, refuse criteria, PAT framing, 'refer to a qualified person') — no mains-repair steps
context: Electrical items are common at community repair events but pose serious safety risks if handled incorrectly. This high-risk content unit provides conservative triage guidance: visual inspection red flags, criteria for refusing an item (damaged cable/plug, signs of burning, modified wiring), conceptual framing of PAT testing (what it is and that it requires a competent person), and a clear 'refer to a qualified electrician' decision rule. Scope is strictly triage-and-referral-only — no instruction to open, rewire, or repair mains-powered appliances. This unit MUST NOT ship without sign-off from the credentialed electrical SME confirmed in research-011 (distinct from the author), and must carry the mandatory disclaimer. Content license: CC-BY-4.0.
objective: Author electrical safety triage notes that are strictly triage-and-referral-only (no mains-repair steps), sourced from paraphrased + cited authoritative references (not verbatim from copyrighted codes), signed off by the credentialed SME from research-011 (distinct from author), and carrying the mandatory 'informational, not a substitute' disclaimer.
acceptanceCriteria: Content is strictly triage-and-referral-only: visual inspection red flags, criteria for refusing an item, conceptual PAT framing, and a 'refer to a qualified electrician' decision rule; zero mains-repair, rewiring, or opening-appliance instruction
expertSignOff.scopeConfirmed is set to 'triage-and-referral-only' in the schema record

The mandatory disclaimer is present verbatim: 'This is general safety information, not a substitute for professional electrical training, inspection, or repair. If in doubt, stop and consult a qualified electrician.'
The content-governance gate passes: the unit has a valid expertSignOff record (name, credential, date, logRef) from the SME confirmed in research-011, who is a different person from the author

All sources are paraphrased and cited (no verbatim copying of copyrighted codes, agency text, or standards body text); no third-party logos or 'Repair Café' name; reusePermitted verified for each source
The unit is published under CC-BY-4.0, renders from the data-002 schema, passes all CI content-governance gates, and prints legibly
output: A committed, schema-valid electrical safety triage notes unit (CC-BY-4.0, riskClass=high) with triage-only scope, mandatory disclaimer, valid expertSignOff record, and a PR-tied review-log entry — passing all CI content-governance gates.
